recommendations.title: Which Is the Best Position to Sleep in?
recommendations.excerpt: If you live anywhere affected by the recent heatwaves, you may well have spent your nights tossing and turning, trying out different sleeping positions in an attempt to get comfortable. But what does the evidence say about which sleeping positions are actually the best?

recommendations.title: Bodyweight Challenge: “I Tried a 4-Week Push up Challenge–Here’s How It Went”
recommendations.excerpt: One writer—who has always struggled with push ups—took on a 30-day challenge to try and master the upper body move. Here’s what happened.

recommendations.title: Reinventing Anna Delvey: How House Arrest, Being Hated and Yearning for Freedom Is Changing the Scammer
recommendations.excerpt: It’s a scorching hot July day in Manhattan, but Anna Delvey wants to walk to her scheduled check-in with New York parole officers. All 40 blocks, that is.

recommendations.title: 5 Fitness Pillars to Stay in Shape for Your Entire Life
recommendations.excerpt: Want stellar health and a killer physique without the forced-march workouts? Get some skin back in the game with these 5 pillars of lifelong fitness.

recommendations.title: Your Clothes Were Never Meant to Fit You
recommendations.excerpt: I was 17 and trying and failing to find a plus-size dress for the prom. Wandering through shops with friends, I’d feign interest in the petite strapless and halter-top gowns before heading toward the rack, sequestered in a back corner, where they kept the dresses in my size.

recommendations.title: Your Breakfast Potatoes Deserve an Upgrade
recommendations.excerpt: Here’s how to make the crispiest, tastiest versions of your favorite breakfast side. No need to choose between home fries or hash browns, here—these recipes cover both and inspire new takes on your morning tubers.

recommendations.title: Diet for a Hotter Climate: Five Plants That Could Help Feed the World
recommendations.excerpt: As the planet warms, these five drought-tolerant and highly nutritious crops offer hope for greater resiliency.

recommendations.title: After Her Son Died In A Hot Car, This Mom Wrote a Memoir About Forgiveness
recommendations.excerpt: “If I can just help one person, that really is what the goal is.”

recommendations.title: Why Nobody Got Paid for One of the Most Sampled Sounds in Hip-Hop
recommendations.excerpt: This month marks the 50th anniversary of hip-hop. A few seconds from one forgotten rap song have tied artists and eras together for decades.

recommendations.title: How Sibling Relationships Change in Adulthood
recommendations.excerpt: As brothers and sisters grow up, what they do can determine whether they stay stuck in their childhood roles—or break free of them.

recommendations.title: The Most Emotional Video Game Music in the Unlikeliest of Places
recommendations.excerpt: The Eleanor Rigby of video game music.

recommendations.title: 9 Recipes to Indulge Your Tastebuds This Tomato Season
recommendations.excerpt: Make the perfect tomato sandwich, mouthwatering honey-roasted tomatoes, or any number of summer meals that celebrate the versatile fruit.

recommendations.title: Coping With Intrusive Thoughts
recommendations.excerpt: Haunted by a reoccurring thought that freaks you out? Intrusive thoughts are more common than you think.

recommendations.title: How Dexterous Thumbs May Have Helped Shape Evolution Two Million Years Ago
recommendations.excerpt: Fossils and biochemical models show tool-wielding hominins used their hands like we do today.

recommendations.title: Stop Being So Hard on Yourself
recommendations.excerpt: Being hard on yourself is not only ineffective, but it is also a hard pattern to break. How can you take a more balanced, emotionally equanimous approach to your performance?

recommendations.title: How to Press Tofu — or Decide If It’s Even Necessary
recommendations.excerpt: Turns out, it doesn’t take much—or anything—to deal with the water in your tofu.

recommendations.title: What Is Amenorrhea and What Do Runners Need to Know About Missing Their Periods?
recommendations.excerpt: It can be a red flag for health problems—but athletes don’t necessarily have to give up running to return to their regular cycles.

recommendations.title: The Radical Roots of Bikesharing
recommendations.excerpt: In mid-1960s Amsterdam, a counterculture movement with a small fleet of white bicycles pioneered a transportation model that’s swept thousands of cities around the world.

recommendations.title: How to Start Meditating
recommendations.excerpt: A gentle yet excuse-busting guide to starting a meditation practice that works for you. Even if that means squeezing in a mindfulness break in the car.

recommendations.title: How to Stock a Pantry
recommendations.excerpt: A shopping list to make your cooking that much better.

recommendations.title: Scientists at Work: Uncovering the Mystery of When and Where Sharks Give Birth
recommendations.excerpt: Researchers are using a newly developed satellite tag to study previously unknown aspects of tiger shark reproduction. This approach could be used on other difficult-to-study shark species.

recommendations.title: How an Expedition to the Galápagos Islands Saved One of the World’s Largest Natural History Museums
recommendations.excerpt: A soon-to-be digitized and publicly accessible collection of specimens helped resurrect the California Academy of Sciences.

recommendations.title: You’re Never Too Old to Regain Lost Muscle. And You Can Do It at Home.
recommendations.excerpt: “Building and maintaining strength is one of the most important things you can do at any stage of life, and it’s extremely important after age 50,” a sports medicine physician said.

recommendations.title: Meet the Retirees Who Live on Cruise Ships
recommendations.excerpt: People ask, ‘don’t you get bored at sea?’” says Janice Yetke, 77. “I say, are you kidding me?"

recommendations.title: The Kitchen Feature That May Decrease Your Home’s Value
recommendations.excerpt: Buyers are looking for something more traditional.

recommendations.title: The Ultimate Dinosaur Reading List
recommendations.excerpt: Triceratops! Stegosaurus! T. rex! Channel your inner five year old with this Brachiosaurus-sized collection of the best articles about dinosaurs, dinosaur fossils, and dinosaur hunters.

recommendations.title: 11 Discontinued Breakfast Cereals
recommendations.excerpt: If you’re craving a nostalgia trip, take a look back at these discontinued cereals from the 1970s, ’80s, and ’90s.

recommendations.title: Take the High Road: the Man Who Visited Every Country in the World – Without Boarding a Plane
recommendations.excerpt: At 34, Torbjørn Pedersen embarked on a seemingly impossible journey that would take 10 years – and involve cerebral malaria and being held up at gunpoint. He reflects on the highs, the lows and the joy of getting married en route.

recommendations.title: The Enduring Myths of ‘Raiders of the Lost Ark’
recommendations.excerpt: Archaeologists look back at what the first Indiana Jones movie got wrong about their profession.

recommendations.title: Big Budgets, Big Headaches
